title: The King Comes Riding
subtitle: Palm Sunday — the multitude descends the Mount of Olives to meet a King upon a donkey's colt

stillness: Be still, and let the road grow quiet beneath you.
He whom you will meet comes humble, riding.
Wait here, on the slope of the Mount of Olives.
gospel: And when Jesus drew near to the descent of the Mount of Olives,
a great multitude, many of those who came down with him,
spread their garments along the way;
others cut green branches from the fruit-bearing trees
with which the Mount of Olives is planted,
and strewed them in the road to adorn it,
lest the beast should strike its foot against a stone.
And the children and the lowly folk of Jerusalem,
when they heard that Jesus was coming,
took branches of olive and of palm,
and went out reverently to meet him,
and received him in honor as a King,
with hymns and songs and great gladness,
and led him on to Jerusalem with praises, singing:
Glory, praise, and honor be to you, O King, Christ the Redeemer.
And the multitudes that went before and that followed cried out:
Hosanna to the Son of David.
Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.
Blessed is the King of Israel.
behold: Behold attentively, as if you were present,
the Lord Jesus coming down from the mountain.
See that he foreknew the crowd would come to meet him,
and yet he mounted a little donkey,
that amid the applause of the running peoples
he might give a wondrous example of humility.
Behold the throngs that go before and follow after,
so that Christ is in the very midst of them.
Watch them tear the green branches from the trees,
and cast their own garments down into the dust,
smoothing the road so the colt will not stumble.
For where the Author of mercy is brought low,
those who have need of mercy must follow in his steps.
consider: Consider what this Hosanna is that they cry.
It is the voice of those who exult and praise together,
or rather of those who beseech and implore;
it shows the affection of the heart more than it names a thing.
Hosanna means: save, I beseech you; O save me.
It is as if they said: O that we might be saved.
To him for whom they cried Save, they confessed a Savior;
for they believed his coming was the salvation of the world.
And when they said, Blessed is he who comes,
they named him singularly free from every sin,
and making others free, who comes in the name of the Father,
not in his own name, but seeking the Father's glory.
Yet when they called him King of Israel, they confessed too little,
for they believed he would reign in time,
and free them from tribute, and from the yoke of Rome.
But he came to reign in the spirit,
and to free the whole human race from the devil —
a thing far greater than the kingdom they desired.
For what great thing was it for the King of ages
to become the King of men?
He is not King of Israel to exact tribute,
nor to arm an army with the sword,
but to rule minds, to counsel us unto eternity,
and to lead the believing, the hoping, the loving
into the kingdom of heaven.
That he willed to be King is condescension, not promotion;
a sign of his mercy, not an increase of his power.
He who on earth is called King of the Jews
is in heaven the Lord of Angels.
prayer: Do not fear; acknowledge him who is praised by you,
and do not tremble when he suffers,
because that blood is poured out
by which your offense may be blotted away,
to you be glory, and praise, and honor.
Save, I beseech you: O save me.

latinJewel.latin: Quod ergo Rex esse voluit Israel, dignatio est non promotio, miserationis indicium, non potestatis augmentum; qui enim appellatus est in terra Rex Judæorum, in cœlis est Dominus Angelorum.
latinJewel.english: That he willed to be King of Israel is condescension, not promotion; a sign of his mercy, not an increase of his power; for he who on earth is called King of the Jews is in heaven the Lord of the Angels.
